conclusion: Default output=batch rowchunk sweep narrows the full-attention grouping rule: rowchunk1 and rowchunk2 are green for c5/c8, rowchunk3 is green only while the non-leading segment has at most two rows (c4/c5) but turns red once the second segment contains rows 3..5 (c6/c7/c8), and rowchunk4/full-native is red as soon as a leading four-row group is formed (c4/c5/c8). The safe diagnostic cap is therefore leading group <=3 and later groups <=2, which is exactly why runtime auto must stay at rowchunk2 for retained correctness until the grouped full-attention producer is fixed. No throughput claim is made.
remaining_blockers: full-native/no-rowchunk and rowchunk>=3 still fail generated-token equality for grouped full-attention
next code target should inspect the native full-attention producer for group-size-sensitive leading chunk size 4 and non-leading chunk size 3+ behavior, not projection dispatch or sampler
